journal_entry.description: Acquisition of land with building for factory construction, financed by 6 annual instalments
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, the land acquisition is recorded at the present value of the payment obligation ($49,173,240 using 6% discount rate for 6 annual payments of $10M). The liability is recorded at the same present value. The existing building to be demolished is capitalized as part of land cost since demolition is necessary to prepare the land for its intended use. The difference between face value ($60M) and present value will be recognized as interest expense over the payment period. Land is not depreciated under IAS 16.

output.reason: Under IFRS, Ford is acquiring land (a non-current asset) for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The debit side records the acquisition of the land asset at its present value. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land acquisition cost (demolition costs are capitalized to land under IFRS). This represents 1 asset increase (Land or Property under development). The financing arrangement (instalments payable) affects only the credit side of the entry.

debit_classifier.reason: Under IFRS, Ford is acquiring land (a non-current asset) for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The debit side records the acquisition of the land asset at its present value. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land acquisition cost (demolition costs are capitalized to land under IFRS). This represents 1 asset increase (Land or Property under development). The financing arrangement (instalments payable) affects only the credit side of the entry.

output.reason: Ford acquires land and building through a deferred payment arrangement. The credit side records the liability for the purchase price payable in instalments. Under IFRS, this creates a financial liability (present value of future payments). This is a liability increase (1 line). The debit side would record the asset (land) at present value and demolition costs would be capitalized to land cost.

credit_classifier.reason: Ford acquires land and building through a deferred payment arrangement. The credit side records the liability for the purchase price payable in instalments. Under IFRS, this creates a financial liability (present value of future payments). This is a liability increase (1 line). The debit side would record the asset (land) at present value and demolition costs would be capitalized to land cost.
